title: NIS2 Annual Security Assessment Completion Tracking

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIS2 Art.21(2)(f) — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(f) (Policies and procedures effectiveness) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.43: NIS2 Annual Security Assessment Completion Tracking.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.43' running on nis2_annual_assessment_register.csv, index=itsm (GRC tasks), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
| inputlookup nis2_annual_assessment_register.csv
| eval due_epoch=strptime(next_assessment_due,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval overdue=if(now()>due_epoch,1,0)
| join legal_entity_id [
    search index=itsm short_description="*NIS2*annual*assessment*" earliest=-400d
    | stats latest(closed_at) as last_closed by company
    | rename company as legal_entity_id
]
| where overdue=1
| table legal_entity_id, assessment_type, next_assessment_due, last_closed, overdue
description: Effectiveness assessments and broader security assessments are recurring obligations. This use case tracks annual independent assessments, red-team summaries, and control testing sign-offs — ensuring each NIS2-scoped entity has a current assessment on file.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIS2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Define assessment types (internal audit, external cert, penetration retest); (2) sync due dates from GRC; (3) alert CFO/legal when entity-level assessment is overdue; (4) attach assessment reports in controlled repository; (5) link findings to remediation in ServiceNow. Indexes required: `index=itsm`; Lookups: `nis2_annual_assessment_register`, `nis2_annual_assessment_register.csv`.
visualization: Table (overdue assessments), Timeline (by entity), Single value (overdue count), Bar chart (assessment type mix).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `nis2_annual_assessment_register.csv`, `index=itsm` (GRC tasks).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Define assessment types (internal audit, external cert, penetration retest); (2) sync due dates from GRC; (3) alert CFO/legal when entity-level assessment is overdue; (4) attach assessment reports in controlled repository; (5) link findings to remediation in ServiceNow.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_annual_assessment_register.csv
| eval due_epoch=strptime(next_assessment_due,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval overdue=if(now()>due_epoch,1,0)
| join legal_entity_id [
    search index=itsm short_description="*NIS2*annual*assessment*" earliest=-400d
    | stats latest(closed_at) as last_closed by company
    | rename company as legal_entity_id
]
| where overdue=1
| table legal_entity_id, assessment_type, next_assessment_due, last_closed, overdue
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIS2 Annual Security Assessment Completion Tracking (Art. 21(2)(f))** — Effectiveness assessments and broader security assessments are recurring obligations. This use case tracks annual independent assessments, red-team summaries, and control testing sign-offs — ensuring each NIS2-scoped entity has a current assessment on file.

Documented **Data sources**: `nis2_annual_assessment_register.csv`, `index=itsm` (GRC tasks). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **due_epoch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **overdue** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where overdue=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: assessment_type, due_epoch, last_closed, legal_entity_id, next_assessment_due, overdue.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (overdue assessments), Timeline (by entity), Single value (overdue count), Bar chart (assessment type mix).
